title: Tower Unite

notes: Everything works perfectly. Game loads much faster than on Windows. Performance is about on par with Windows.
title: Tower Unite

notes: The dev team got rid of the empty linux repo and decided to semi-officially support linux through Proton, so this is no longer borked. That said, there are some tweaks you'll need to get the game running seamlessly: Opt-in to the Steam Client Beta (can't verify this makes a difference, but included for completeness); Set the Proton version, either globally or just for this game, to 3.16-4; Boot, then quit; Run winecfg for this game's prefix (default location: /home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/394690/pfx/); Search for and add the "xaudio2_7" library, apply changes; Run game in DirectX 10 mode. Game runs relatively well, but the performance hit from Proton required me to lower my graphics settings. Aside from that, everything I tested worked correctly: Main Menu, Plaza, Typing Derby, Ball Race, Minigolf, Character Customization, Workshop Player Models
title: Tower Unite

notes: Infinite loading screen when joining lobby without disabling Esync. Frequent crashes and graphical glitches with Esync disabled.
title: Tower Unite

notes: No issues. Both DX11 and DX10 modes work for me with no tweaks needed. Running highest settings at 1080p. Frames can dip when a lot of players are on, but that's expected of any system. I've had a couple crashes, but I don't think that's exclusive to Proton as my windows friend has crashed just as much as I have.

notes: Everything appears to work perfectly. The newly launched Proton 4.2-1 fixed audio issues with Tower Unite where the games sound was either blasting loud or not working properly. Also make sure to run stable Mesa drivers for AMD graphics cards for this title or you might experience crashing like I did using Mesa 19.1.
title: Tower Unite

notes: With the latest version of Proton trying to type crashes the game, running it back to 4.2-9 seems to fix it. I have issues with media players not loading videos properly sometimes, but it's fixed by restarting the game with the renderer you weren't using.
title: Tower Unite

notes:
I earlier reported the game as 'Borked' because I was unable to get it to start. Turns out the game will not launch if it is installed on a NTFS drive. If you cannot launch the game, make sure it's not installed on NTFS. Ext4 works perfectly. I have not had any of the video player problems reported by others.

I would also recommend using DirectX 10 mode, as I have had problems with crashes in the default mode. If you use Lutris, you can do this by adding "-dxlevel 100" as an argument in the game's settings.
title: Tower Unite
